title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall on May 22, marking a decade since his last Pops appearance.
The concert featured American music with pieces by composers like Copland and Bernstein, and included Odom Jr.'s renditions of Broadway hits.
Odom Jr. delivered performances of classics such as 'Fly Me To The Moon' and 'Wait For It', showcasing his theatrical and vocal prowess.

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda of Chelsea faces multiple charges for assaulting two children, including strangulation and use of dangerous weapons.
Incidents involved burning a child with a lighter and another with a heated fork; assaults were allegedly caught on video.
Bautista-Zepeda was apprehended in Virginia after fleeing Massachusetts; arraigned with a dangerousness hearing set for May 28.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, convicted of raping a 20-year-old in a Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023.
Jammal sentenced to 13 ½ to 15 years in prison for rape, plus 5 years probation for assault.
Incident occurred on July 9, 2023; victim confronted Jammal, who then fled before police arrived.

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Newton's 1893 Church of the Open Word will be converted into condos after city approval.
The project includes transforming the church into six units and building a separate 26-unit condo building.
Habitech Communities purchased the property in 2024 for $4 million, planning residential conversion.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Boston Celtics could target Kawhi Leonard as he may become available for trade, fitting their defensive and scoring needs.
LeBron James is an unrestricted free agent and could be a fit for the Celtics if he leaves Los Angeles.
John Collins, Jaxson Hayes, and Coby White are additional targets to enhance Celtics' depth in forward and guard positions.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to the NCAA Division II World Series for the first time after defeating Molloy University.
Endicott College reached the NCAA Division III championship round for the fourth consecutive year by beating Salve Regina University.
Bentley won its decisive Game 3 with a walk-off score by Stan DeMartinis, while Endicott secured a Game 3 victory with a 12-4 score.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Celtics plan to work out UConn star Alex Karaban next week ahead of the 2026 NBA Draft.
Karaban, from Southborough, won two NCAA championships at UConn and was named to the All-Big East First-team.
Karaban ranks sixth in scoring in UConn history and is the program's all-time leader in several categories.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth performed at MGM Music Hall, blending jazz and pop, with a focus on musicianship over spectacle.
The concert included songs from Puth’s album 'Whatever’s Clever!' and older hits, prompting strong reactions from the audience.
Puth's tour spans 22 countries, concluding in Australia, combining musical innovation with nostalgic performances.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers played 'Bringing Down the Horse' in full during their concert at The Cabot in Beverly on May 22, 2026.
Jakob Dylan's band followed their album performance with a cover of Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark' album tracks.
The concert included Wallflowers hits like 'One Headlight' and concluded with Petty favorites 'Refugee' and 'The Waiting'.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston saw the largest population decline in Massachusetts, losing 1,338 residents, now totaling 672,973.
Everett and Worcester reported the biggest population gains, with increases of 2,362 and 1,916 residents, respectively.
High housing costs and reduced immigration are contributing to Boston's population decrease, according to experts.
